label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 19

extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won a controlled sprint after Trek-Segafredo paced hard enough over two mountain laps to shed most sprinters but preserved their own finisher
tactical_patterns: Trek paced Cat-2 climb twice specifically to eliminate pure sprinters while keeping Pedersen, filtering field to manageable size.
Scotson attacked early from reduced group knowing Trek's dominance meant only hope was catching Pedersen before launch.
Three-rider breakaway granted no meaningful gap despite modest size, suggesting peloton calculated sprint finish certainty on double-lap profile.

rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: third stage win after early-race second places suggests form improvement through three-week race rather than decline
rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: positive: survived Trek's selective mountain pace as pure sprinter, indicating climbing improvement or misjudged categorization
rider_signals.VERMEERSCH Gianni: neutral: fast finish from reduced group confirms he's not eliminated by moderate climbing but can't match top-tier speed
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: gained only two seconds on rest day eve before queen stage suggests team prioritizing defense over aggression
